tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "echo:/YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "echo:/XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

in.description: Scope the query to children of specific objects (transitively). Use this to query items within containers such as feeds or folders. For example, to find emails use `in: [mailbox1.feed, mailbox2.feed]`.

typename.description: The typename of the objects to list.

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

content.content:
You are an agent running in the non-interactive mode.
The user is unable to see what you are doing, and cannot answer any questions.
Do not ask questions.
Complete the task before you, and at the end call [completeJob] with the output.
If you are unable to complete the task, call [completeJob] with the failure reason.
If no output is required, call [completeJob] with an empty object: {}
Do not stop until you call [completeJob].
You are running within a test environment.
The prompt is the specification for the test.
Perform the instructions precisely and do not deviate.
Do not fake any work if the provided tools don't work.
The goal is to test the system, so be honest about the results.
If available tools prevented you from completing the task fully, report the failure.
Do not fall back on your own knowledge, only use the tools provided.

## Skills Definitions

<skill>
  You can query available skills and enable them in the current conversation.
  Use [query-skills] to refresh the list of available skills.
  Use [enable-skills] to enable skills by their keys. Always call [query-skills] first.
  Only skills with agentCanEnable=true can be enabled by the agent.
  
  <available_skills>
  -  "Agent skill"
  -  "Agent Wizard" -- Help the user create a new agent (subscriptions, optional cron timer, sync-triggers after edits). [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Alarm" -- Schedule a self-wake and inspect the current time. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Assistant"
  -  "Browser" -- Access to a real isolated browser. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Calendar"
  -  "Connectors" -- Prompt the user to connect external services when a connector is required but unavailable. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Database" -- Query and manipulate objects in the ECHO database. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Delegation" -- Delegates work to sub-agents and tracks it as plan tasks.
  -  "Discord" -- Discord integration.
  -  "Inbox" [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Inbox (Send)"
  -  "Linear" -- Syncs Linear workspaces.
  -  "Markdown" -- Work with markdown documents. Preferred over raw database operations. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Memory" -- Persistent memory storage and retrieval. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Planning" -- Plans and tracks complex tasks using artifacts. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Skill Manager" -- Query and enable skills in the current conversation.
  -  "Triggers" -- Trigger management and automation.
  -  "Web Search" -- Search the web. [agent-can-enable]
  </available_skills>
  
  NOTE: You must enable the skill to use it, only then the tools from that skill will appear.
</skill>

<skill>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</skill>

Draft a new document.
Pick a title and content yourself.

Completion criteria:
- A Markdown document object exists in the database.
- Agent enabled the markdown skill.
- Agent used markdown skill tools to draft a document.
<input>{}</input>

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "echo:/YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "echo:/XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

content.content:
You are an agent running in the non-interactive mode.
The user is unable to see what you are doing, and cannot answer any questions.
Do not ask questions.
Complete the task before you, and at the end call [completeJob] with the output.
If you are unable to complete the task, call [completeJob] with the failure reason.
If no output is required, call [completeJob] with an empty object: {}
Do not stop until you call [completeJob].
You are running within a test environment.
The prompt is the specification for the test.
Perform the instructions precisely and do not deviate.
Do not fake any work if the provided tools don't work.
The goal is to test the system, so be honest about the results.
If available tools prevented you from completing the task fully, report the failure.
Do not fall back on your own knowledge, only use the tools provided.

## Skills Definitions

<skill>
  You can query available skills and enable them in the current conversation.
  Use [query-skills] to refresh the list of available skills.
  Use [enable-skills] to enable skills by their keys. Always call [query-skills] first.
  Only skills with agentCanEnable=true can be enabled by the agent.
  
  <available_skills>
  -  "Agent skill"
  -  "Agent Wizard" -- Help the user create a new agent (subscriptions, optional cron timer, sync-triggers after edits). [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Alarm" -- Schedule a self-wake and inspect the current time. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Assistant"
  -  "Browser" -- Access to a real isolated browser. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Calendar"
  -  "Connectors" -- Prompt the user to connect external services when a connector is required but unavailable. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Database" -- Query and manipulate objects in the ECHO database. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Delegation" -- Delegates work to sub-agents and tracks it as plan tasks.
  -  "Discord" -- Discord integration.
  -  "Inbox" [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Inbox (Send)"
  -  "Linear" -- Syncs Linear workspaces.
  -  "Markdown" -- Work with markdown documents. Preferred over raw database operations. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Memory" -- Persistent memory storage and retrieval. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Planning" -- Plans and tracks complex tasks using artifacts. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Skill Manager" -- Query and enable skills in the current conversation.
  -  "Triggers" -- Trigger management and automation.
  -  "Web Search" -- Search the web. [agent-can-enable]
  </available_skills>
  
  NOTE: You must enable the skill to use it, only then the tools from that skill will appear.
</skill>

<skill>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</skill>

<skill>
  You can create, read and update markdown documents.
  When asked to edit or update documents return updates as a set of compact diff string pairs.
  For each diff, respond with the smallest possible matching span.
  
  Example:
  ```diff
  - "There is a tyop in this sentence."
  + "There is a typo in this sentence."
  - "This id good."
  + "This sentence is really good."
  ```
</skill>

Draft a new document.
Pick a title and content yourself.

Completion criteria:
- A Markdown document object exists in the database.
- Agent enabled the markdown skill.
- Agent used markdown skill tools to draft a document.

params.name: The Art of Deep Work
params.content:
# The Art of Deep Work

Deep work is the ability to focus without distraction on a cognitively demanding task. In an age of constant notifications and fragmented attention, cultivating this skill has become both increasingly rare and increasingly valuable.

## Why It Matters

The capacity to concentrate intensely allows us to learn complex subjects quickly and produce work of exceptional quality. When we give a task our undivided attention, we unlock a level of insight and craftsmanship that shallow, distracted effort can never reach.

## Building the Habit

- **Schedule focus blocks.** Reserve uninterrupted time on your calendar and treat it as a non-negotiable appointment.
- **Remove distractions.** Silence your phone, close unnecessary tabs, and create a quiet environment.
- **Start small.** Begin with 30-minute sessions and gradually extend your endurance.
- **Reflect and refine.** At the end of each session, note what worked and what pulled you away.

## Conclusion

Deep work is not a talent reserved for a select few—it is a discipline anyone can develop. By protecting your attention and practicing sustained focus, you can accomplish more meaningful work in less time.

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "echo:/YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "echo:/XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

in.description: Scope the query to children of specific objects (transitively). Use this to query items within containers such as feeds or folders. For example, to find emails use `in: [mailbox1.feed, mailbox2.feed]`.

typename.description: The typename of the objects to list.

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

params.name: The Art of Deep Work
params.content:
# The Art of Deep Work

Deep work is the ability to focus without distraction on a cognitively demanding task. In an age of constant notifications and fragmented attention, cultivating this skill has become both increasingly rare and increasingly valuable.

## Why It Matters

The capacity to concentrate intensely allows us to learn complex subjects quickly and produce work of exceptional quality. When we give a task our undivided attention, we unlock a level of insight and craftsmanship that shallow, distracted effort can never reach.

## Building the Habit

- **Schedule focus blocks.** Reserve uninterrupted time on your calendar and treat it as a non-negotiable appointment.
- **Remove distractions.** Silence your phone, close unnecessary tabs, and create a quiet environment.
- **Start small.** Begin with 30-minute sessions and gradually extend your endurance.
- **Reflect and refine.** At the end of each session, note what worked and what pulled you away.

## Conclusion

Deep work is not a talent reserved for a select few—it is a discipline anyone can develop. By protecting your attention and practicing sustained focus, you can accomplish more meaningful work in less time.

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "echo:/YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "echo:/XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

in.description: Scope the query to children of specific objects (transitively). Use this to query items within containers such as feeds or folders. For example, to find emails use `in: [mailbox1.feed, mailbox2.feed]`.

typename.description: The typename of the objects to list.

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

Do not fake any work if the provided tools don't work.

If available tools prevented you from completing the task fully, report the failure.
Do not fall back on your own knowledge, only use the tools provided.

  You can query available skills and enable them in the current conversation.
  Use [query-skills] to refresh the list of available skills.
  Use [enable-skills] to enable skills by their keys. Always call [query-skills] first.
  Only skills with agentCanEnable=true can be enabled by the agent.
  
  <available_skills>
  -  "Agent skill"
  -  "Agent Wizard" -- Help the user create a new agent (subscriptions, optional cron timer, sync-triggers after edits). [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Alarm" -- Schedule a self-wake and inspect the current time. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Assistant"
  -  "Browser" -- Access to a real isolated browser. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Calendar"

  -  "Skill Manager" -- Query and enable skills in the current conversation.
  -  "Triggers" -- Trigger management and automation.
  -  "Web Search" -- Search the web. [agent-can-enable]
  </available_skills>
  
  NOTE: You must enable the skill to use it, only then the tools from that skill will appear.
</skill>

<skill>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</skill>

<skill>
  You can create, read and update markdown documents.
  When asked to edit or update documents return updates as a set of compact diff string pairs.
  For each diff, respond with the smallest possible matching span.
  
  Example:
  ```diff
  - "There is a tyop in this sentence."
  + "There is a typo in this sentence."
  - "This id good."
  + "This sentence is really good."
  ```
</skill>

for markdown.

ers what deep work is, why it matters, how to build the habit, and a concluding thought.

the document exists in the database by opening it and confirming its content.

You are an agent running in the non-interactive mode.
The user is unable to see what you are doing, and cannot answer any questions.
Do not ask questions.
Complete the task before you, and at the end call [completeJob] with the output.
If you are unable to complete the task, call [completeJob] with the failure reason.
If no output is required, call [completeJob] with an empty object: {}
Do not stop until you call [completeJob].
You are running within a test environment.
The prompt is the specification for the test.
Perform the instructions precisely and do not deviate.
Do not fake any work if the provided tools don't work.
The goal is to test the system, so be honest about the results.
If available tools prevented you from completing the task fully, report the failure.
Do not fall back on your own knowledge, only use the tools provided.

## Skills Definitions

<skill>
  You can query available skills and enable them in the current conversation.
  Use [query-skills] to refresh the list of available skills.
  Use [enable-skills] to enable skills by their keys. Always call [query-skills] first.
  Only skills with agentCanEnable=true can be enabled by the agent.
  
  <available_skills>
  -  "Agent skill"
  -  "Agent Wizard" -- Help the user create a new agent (subscriptions, optional cron timer, sync-triggers after edits). [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Alarm" -- Schedule a self-wake and inspect the current time. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Assistant"
  -  "Browser" -- Access to a real isolated browser. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Calendar"
  -  "Connectors" -- Prompt the user to connect external services when a connector is required but unavailable. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Database" -- Query and manipulate objects in the ECHO database. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Delegation" -- Delegates work to sub-agents and tracks it as plan tasks.
  -  "Discord" -- Discord integration.
  -  "Inbox" [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Inbox (Send)"
  -  "Linear" -- Syncs Linear workspaces.
  -  "Markdown" -- Work with markdown documents. Preferred over raw database operations. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Memory" -- Persistent memory storage and retrieval. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Planning" -- Plans and tracks complex tasks using artifacts. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Skill Manager" -- Query and enable skills in the current conversation.
  -  "Triggers" -- Trigger management and automation.
  -  "Web Search" -- Search the web. [agent-can-enable]
  </available_skills>
  
  NOTE: You must enable the skill to use it, only then the tools from that skill will appear.
</skill>

<skill>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</skill>

without providing oldString (omit oldString entirely — do not pass an empty string):
"Hello from an empty document."

Open the document again and confirm the content is exactly "Hello from an empty document."

- Agent enabled the markdown skill successfully.
- A markdown document was created with initially empty content.
- The Update operation completed without error when appending with no oldString.
- Final document content is exactly "Hello from an empty document."

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "echo:/YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "echo:/XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

content.content:
You are an agent running in the non-interactive mode.
The user is unable to see what you are doing, and cannot answer any questions.
Do not ask questions.
Complete the task before you, and at the end call [completeJob] with the output.
If you are unable to complete the task, call [completeJob] with the failure reason.
If no output is required, call [completeJob] with an empty object: {}
Do not stop until you call [completeJob].
You are running within a test environment.
The prompt is the specification for the test.
Perform the instructions precisely and do not deviate.
Do not fake any work if the provided tools don't work.
The goal is to test the system, so be honest about the results.
If available tools prevented you from completing the task fully, report the failure.
Do not fall back on your own knowledge, only use the tools provided.

## Skills Definitions

<skill>
  You can query available skills and enable them in the current conversation.
  Use [query-skills] to refresh the list of available skills.
  Use [enable-skills] to enable skills by their keys. Always call [query-skills] first.
  Only skills with agentCanEnable=true can be enabled by the agent.
  
  <available_skills>
  -  "Agent skill"
  -  "Agent Wizard" -- Help the user create a new agent (subscriptions, optional cron timer, sync-triggers after edits). [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Alarm" -- Schedule a self-wake and inspect the current time. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Assistant"
  -  "Browser" -- Access to a real isolated browser. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Calendar"
  -  "Connectors" -- Prompt the user to connect external services when a connector is required but unavailable. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Database" -- Query and manipulate objects in the ECHO database. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Delegation" -- Delegates work to sub-agents and tracks it as plan tasks.
  -  "Discord" -- Discord integration.
  -  "Inbox" [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Inbox (Send)"
  -  "Linear" -- Syncs Linear workspaces.
  -  "Markdown" -- Work with markdown documents. Preferred over raw database operations. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Memory" -- Persistent memory storage and retrieval. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Planning" -- Plans and tracks complex tasks using artifacts. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Skill Manager" -- Query and enable skills in the current conversation.
  -  "Triggers" -- Trigger management and automation.
  -  "Web Search" -- Search the web. [agent-can-enable]
  </available_skills>
  
  NOTE: You must enable the skill to use it, only then the tools from that skill will appear.
</skill>

<skill>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</skill>

<skill>
  You can create, read and update markdown documents.
  When asked to edit or update documents return updates as a set of compact diff string pairs.
  For each diff, respond with the smallest possible matching span.
  
  Example:
  ```diff
  - "There is a tyop in this sentence."
  + "There is a typo in this sentence."
  - "This id good."
  + "This sentence is really good."
  ```
</skill>

The database starts empty.

Enable the markdown skill (key: org.dxos.skill.markdown) using the skill manager.

Create a new markdown document named "Empty Notes" with empty content (no body text).

Open the document and confirm its content is empty.

Use the markdown Update operation (org.dxos.function.markdown.update) to append this exact line
without providing oldString (omit oldString entirely — do not pass an empty string):
"Hello from an empty document."

Open the document again and confirm the content is exactly "Hello from an empty document."

Completion criteria:
- Agent enabled the markdown skill successfully.
- A markdown document was created with initially empty content.
- The Update operation completed without error when appending with no oldString.
- Final document content is exactly "Hello from an empty document."
<input>{}</input>

content.text: Skill enabled. Now creating the document with empty content.

required: Agent enabled the markdown skill successfully.
A markdown document was created with initially empty content.
The Update operation completed without error when appending with no oldString.
Final document content is exactly "Hello from an empty document."
completedCriteria.description: True/false for passed or not passed completion criteria.

description.description: Optional longer message describing in detail what went wrong

  -  "Calendar"

  -  "Database" -- Query and manipulate objects in the ECHO database. [agent-can-enable]

  -  "Discord" -- Discord integration.

Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.

Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "echo:/YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "echo:/XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

content.content:
You are an agent running in the non-interactive mode.
The user is unable to see what you are doing, and cannot answer any questions.
Do not ask questions.
Complete the task before you, and at the end call [completeJob] with the output.
If you are unable to complete the task, call [completeJob] with the failure reason.
If no output is required, call [completeJob] with an empty object: {}
Do not stop until you call [completeJob].
You are running within a test environment.
The prompt is the specification for the test.
Perform the instructions precisely and do not deviate.
Do not fake any work if the provided tools don't work.
The goal is to test the system, so be honest about the results.
If available tools prevented you from completing the task fully, report the failure.
Do not fall back on your own knowledge, only use the tools provided.

## Skills Definitions

<skill>
  You can query available skills and enable them in the current conversation.
  Use [query-skills] to refresh the list of available skills.
  Use [enable-skills] to enable skills by their keys. Always call [query-skills] first.
  Only skills with agentCanEnable=true can be enabled by the agent.
  
  <available_skills>
  -  "Agent skill"
  -  "Agent Wizard" -- Help the user create a new agent (subscriptions, optional cron timer, sync-triggers after edits). [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Alarm" -- Schedule a self-wake and inspect the current time. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Assistant"
  -  "Browser" -- Access to a real isolated browser. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Calendar"
  -  "Connectors" -- Prompt the user to connect external services when a connector is required but unavailable. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Database" -- Query and manipulate objects in the ECHO database. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Delegation" -- Delegates work to sub-agents and tracks it as plan tasks.
  -  "Discord" -- Discord integration.
  -  "Inbox" [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Inbox (Send)"
  -  "Linear" -- Syncs Linear workspaces.
  -  "Markdown" -- Work with markdown documents. Preferred over raw database operations. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Memory" -- Persistent memory storage and retrieval. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Planning" -- Plans and tracks complex tasks using artifacts. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Skill Manager" -- Query and enable skills in the current conversation.
  -  "Triggers" -- Trigger management and automation.
  -  "Web Search" -- Search the web. [agent-can-enable]
  </available_skills>
  
  NOTE: You must enable the skill to use it, only then the tools from that skill will appear.
</skill>

<skill>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</skill>

<skill>
  You can create, read and update markdown documents.
  When asked to edit or update documents return updates as a set of compact diff string pairs.
  For each diff, respond with the smallest possible matching span.
  
  Example:
  ```diff
  - "There is a tyop in this sentence."
  + "There is a typo in this sentence."
  - "This id good."
  + "This sentence is really good."
  ```
</skill>

The database starts empty.

Enable the markdown skill (key: org.dxos.skill.markdown) using the skill manager.

Create a new markdown document named "Empty Notes" with empty content (no body text).

Open the document and confirm its content is empty.

Use the markdown Update operation (org.dxos.function.markdown.update) to append this exact line
without providing oldString (omit oldString entirely — do not pass an empty string):
"Hello from an empty document."

Open the document again and confirm the content is exactly "Hello from an empty document."

Completion criteria:
- Agent enabled the markdown skill successfully.
- A markdown document was created with initially empty content.
- The Update operation completed without error when appending with no oldString.
- Final document content is exactly "Hello from an empty document."

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "echo:/YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "echo:/XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "echo:/YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "echo:/XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

content.content:
You are an agent running in the non-interactive mode.
The user is unable to see what you are doing, and cannot answer any questions.
Do not ask questions.
Complete the task before you, and at the end call [completeJob] with the output.
If you are unable to complete the task, call [completeJob] with the failure reason.
If no output is required, call [completeJob] with an empty object: {}
Do not stop until you call [completeJob].
You are running within a test environment.
The prompt is the specification for the test.
Perform the instructions precisely and do not deviate.
Do not fake any work if the provided tools don't work.
The goal is to test the system, so be honest about the results.
If available tools prevented you from completing the task fully, report the failure.
Do not fall back on your own knowledge, only use the tools provided.

## Skills Definitions

<skill>
  You can query available skills and enable them in the current conversation.
  Use [query-skills] to refresh the list of available skills.
  Use [enable-skills] to enable skills by their keys. Always call [query-skills] first.
  Only skills with agentCanEnable=true can be enabled by the agent.
  
  <available_skills>
  -  "Agent skill"
  -  "Agent Wizard" -- Help the user create a new agent (subscriptions, optional cron timer, sync-triggers after edits). [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Alarm" -- Schedule a self-wake and inspect the current time. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Assistant"
  -  "Browser" -- Access to a real isolated browser. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Calendar"
  -  "Connectors" -- Prompt the user to connect external services when a connector is required but unavailable. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Database" -- Query and manipulate objects in the ECHO database. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Delegation" -- Delegates work to sub-agents and tracks it as plan tasks.
  -  "Discord" -- Discord integration.
  -  "Inbox" [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Inbox (Send)"
  -  "Linear" -- Syncs Linear workspaces.
  -  "Markdown" -- Work with markdown documents. Preferred over raw database operations. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Memory" -- Persistent memory storage and retrieval. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Planning" -- Plans and tracks complex tasks using artifacts. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Skill Manager" -- Query and enable skills in the current conversation.
  -  "Triggers" -- Trigger management and automation.
  -  "Web Search" -- Search the web. [agent-can-enable]
  </available_skills>
  
  NOTE: You must enable the skill to use it, only then the tools from that skill will appear.
</skill>

<skill>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</skill>

<skill>
  You can create, read and update markdown documents.
  When asked to edit or update documents return updates as a set of compact diff string pairs.
  For each diff, respond with the smallest possible matching span.
  
  Example:
  ```diff
  - "There is a tyop in this sentence."
  + "There is a typo in this sentence."
  - "This id good."
  + "This sentence is really good."
  ```
</skill>

The database starts empty.

Enable the markdown skill (key: org.dxos.skill.markdown) using the skill manager.

Create a new markdown document named "Empty Notes" with empty content (no body text).

Open the document and confirm its content is empty.

Use the markdown Update operation (org.dxos.function.markdown.update) to append this exact line
without providing oldString (omit oldString entirely — do not pass an empty string):
"Hello from an empty document."

Open the document again and confirm the content is exactly "Hello from an empty document."

Completion criteria:
- Agent enabled the markdown skill successfully.
- A markdown document was created with initially empty content.
- The Update operation completed without error when appending with no oldString.
- Final document content is exactly "Hello from an empty document."

content.content:
You are an agent running in the non-interactive mode.
The user is unable to see what you are doing, and cannot answer any questions.
Do not ask questions.
Complete the task before you, and at the end call [completeJob] with the output.
If you are unable to complete the task, call [completeJob] with the failure reason.
If no output is required, call [completeJob] with an empty object: {}
Do not stop until you call [completeJob].
You are running within a test environment.
The prompt is the specification for the test.
Perform the instructions precisely and do not deviate.
Do not fake any work if the provided tools don't work.
The goal is to test the system, so be honest about the results.
If available tools prevented you from completing the task fully, report the failure.
Do not fall back on your own knowledge, only use the tools provided.

## Skills Definitions

<skill>
  You can query available skills and enable them in the current conversation.
  Use [query-skills] to refresh the list of available skills.
  Use [enable-skills] to enable skills by their keys. Always call [query-skills] first.
  Only skills with agentCanEnable=true can be enabled by the agent.
  
  <available_skills>
  -  "Agent skill"
  -  "Agent Wizard" -- Help the user create a new agent (subscriptions, optional cron timer, sync-triggers after edits). [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Alarm" -- Schedule a self-wake and inspect the current time. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Assistant"
  -  "Browser" -- Access to a real isolated browser. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Calendar"
  -  "Connectors" -- Prompt the user to connect external services when a connector is required but unavailable. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Database" -- Query and manipulate objects in the ECHO database. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Delegation" -- Delegates work to sub-agents and tracks it as plan tasks.
  -  "Discord" -- Discord integration.
  -  "Inbox" [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Inbox (Send)"
  -  "Linear" -- Syncs Linear workspaces.
  -  "Markdown" -- Work with markdown documents. Preferred over raw database operations. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Memory" -- Persistent memory storage and retrieval. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Planning" -- Plans and tracks complex tasks using artifacts. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Skill Manager" -- Query and enable skills in the current conversation.
  -  "Triggers" -- Trigger management and automation.
  -  "Web Search" -- Search the web. [agent-can-enable]
  </available_skills>
  
  NOTE: You must enable the skill to use it, only then the tools from that skill will appear.
</skill>

<skill>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</skill>

<skill>
  You can create, read and update markdown documents.
  When asked to edit or update documents return updates as a set of compact diff string pairs.
  For each diff, respond with the smallest possible matching span.
  
  Example:
  ```diff
  - "There is a tyop in this sentence."
  + "There is a typo in this sentence."
  - "This id good."
  + "This sentence is really good."
  ```
</skill>

The database starts empty.

Enable the markdown skill (key: org.dxos.skill.markdown) using the skill manager.

Create a new markdown document named "Empty Notes" with empty content (no body text).

Open the document and confirm its content is empty.

Use the markdown Update operation (org.dxos.function.markdown.update) to append this exact line
without providing oldString (omit oldString entirely — do not pass an empty string):
"Hello from an empty document."

Open the document again and confirm the content is exactly "Hello from an empty document."

Completion criteria:
- Agent enabled the markdown skill successfully.
- A markdown document was created with initially empty content.
- The Update operation completed without error when appending with no oldString.
- Final document content is exactly "Hello from an empty document."

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "echo:/YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "echo:/XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

in.description: Scope the query to children of specific objects (transitively). Use this to query items within containers such as feeds or folders. For example, to find emails use `in: [mailbox1.feed, mailbox2.feed]`.

typename.description: The typename of the objects to list.

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "echo:/YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "echo:/XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

in.description: Scope the query to children of specific objects (transitively). Use this to query items within containers such as feeds or folders. For example, to find emails use `in: [mailbox1.feed, mailbox2.feed]`.

typename.description: The typename of the objects to list.

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

content.content:
You are an agent running in the non-interactive mode.
The user is unable to see what you are doing, and cannot answer any questions.
Do not ask questions.
Complete the task before you, and at the end call [completeJob] with the output.
If you are unable to complete the task, call [completeJob] with the failure reason.
If no output is required, call [completeJob] with an empty object: {}
Do not stop until you call [completeJob].
You are running within a test environment.
The prompt is the specification for the test.
Perform the instructions precisely and do not deviate.
Do not fake any work if the provided tools don't work.
The goal is to test the system, so be honest about the results.
If available tools prevented you from completing the task fully, report the failure.
Do not fall back on your own knowledge, only use the tools provided.

## Skills Definitions

<skill>
  You can query available skills and enable them in the current conversation.
  Use [query-skills] to refresh the list of available skills.
  Use [enable-skills] to enable skills by their keys. Always call [query-skills] first.
  Only skills with agentCanEnable=true can be enabled by the agent.
  
  <available_skills>
  -  "Agent skill"
  -  "Agent Wizard" -- Help the user create a new agent (subscriptions, optional cron timer, sync-triggers after edits). [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Alarm" -- Schedule a self-wake and inspect the current time. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Assistant"
  -  "Browser" -- Access to a real isolated browser. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Calendar"
  -  "Connectors" -- Prompt the user to connect external services when a connector is required but unavailable. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Database" -- Query and manipulate objects in the ECHO database. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Delegation" -- Delegates work to sub-agents and tracks it as plan tasks.
  -  "Discord" -- Discord integration.
  -  "Inbox" [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Inbox (Send)"
  -  "Linear" -- Syncs Linear workspaces.
  -  "Markdown" -- Work with markdown documents. Preferred over raw database operations. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Memory" -- Persistent memory storage and retrieval. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Planning" -- Plans and tracks complex tasks using artifacts. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Skill Manager" -- Query and enable skills in the current conversation.
  -  "Triggers" -- Trigger management and automation.
  -  "Web Search" -- Search the web. [agent-can-enable]
  </available_skills>
  
  NOTE: You must enable the skill to use it, only then the tools from that skill will appear.
</skill>

<skill>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</skill>

<skill>
  You can create, read and update markdown documents.
  When asked to edit or update documents return updates as a set of compact diff string pairs.
  For each diff, respond with the smallest possible matching span.
  
  Example:
  ```diff
  - "There is a tyop in this sentence."
  + "There is a typo in this sentence."
  - "This id good."
  + "This sentence is really good."
  ```
</skill>

The database starts empty.

Enable the markdown skill (key: org.dxos.skill.markdown) using the skill manager.

Create a new markdown document named "Empty Notes" with empty content (no body text).

Open the document and confirm its content is empty.

Use the markdown Update operation (org.dxos.function.markdown.update) to append this exact line
without providing oldString (omit oldString entirely — do not pass an empty string):
"Hello from an empty document."

Open the document again and confirm the content is exactly "Hello from an empty document."

Completion criteria:
- Agent enabled the markdown skill successfully.
- A markdown document was created with initially empty content.
- The Update operation completed without error when appending with no oldString.
- Final document content is exactly "Hello from an empty document."
<input>{}</input>

1. ✅ Enabled the markdown skill (org.dxos.skill.markdown) via the skill manager.
2. ✅ Created a markdown document named "Empty Notes" with empty content.
3. ✅ Opened it and confirmed content was empty ("").
4. ✅ Ran the Update operation appending "Hello from an empty document." with no oldString provided — completed without error.
5. ✅ Reopened and confirmed the final content is exactly "Hello from an empty document."

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "echo:/YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "echo:/XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

in.description: Scope the query to children of specific objects (transitively). Use this to query items within containers such as feeds or folders. For example, to find emails use `in: [mailbox1.feed, mailbox2.feed]`.

typename.description: The typename of the objects to list.

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

content.content:
You are an agent running in the non-interactive mode.
The user is unable to see what you are doing, and cannot answer any questions.
Do not ask questions.
Complete the task before you, and at the end call [completeJob] with the output.
If you are unable to complete the task, call [completeJob] with the failure reason.
If no output is required, call [completeJob] with an empty object: {}
Do not stop until you call [completeJob].
You are running within a test environment.
The prompt is the specification for the test.
Perform the instructions precisely and do not deviate.
Do not fake any work if the provided tools don't work.
The goal is to test the system, so be honest about the results.
If available tools prevented you from completing the task fully, report the failure.
Do not fall back on your own knowledge, only use the tools provided.

## Skills Definitions

  You can query available skills and enable them in the current conversation.

  Use [enable-skills] to enable skills by their keys. Always call [query-skills] first.
  Only skills with agentCanEnable=true can be enabled by the agent.

  -  "Database" -- Query and manipulate objects in the ECHO database. [agent-can-enable]

  -  "Discord" -- Discord integration.
  -  "Inbox" [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Inbox (Send)"
  -  "Linear" -- Syncs Linear workspaces.
  -  "Markdown" -- Work with markdown documents. Preferred over raw database operations. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Memory" -- Persistent memory storage and retrieval. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Planning" -- Plans and tracks complex tasks using artifacts. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Skill Manager" -- Query and enable skills in the current conversation.
  -  "Triggers" -- Trigger management and automation.
  -  "Web Search" -- Search the web. [agent-can-enable]
  </available_skills>

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "echo:/YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "echo:/XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

in.description: Scope the query to children of specific objects (transitively). Use this to query items within containers such as feeds or folders. For example, to find emails use `in: [mailbox1.feed, mailbox2.feed]`.

typename.description: The typename of the objects to list.

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

params.name: The Art of Deep Work
params.content:
# The Art of Deep Work

Deep work is the ability to focus without distraction on a cognitively demanding task. In an age of constant notifications and fragmented attention, cultivating this skill has become both increasingly rare and increasingly valuable.

## Why It Matters

- **Quality over quantity**: Sustained focus produces work of higher quality than scattered effort.
- **Skill acquisition**: Mastering hard things requires uninterrupted concentration.
- **Meaning**: Immersion in challenging work is a reliable source of satisfaction.

## Principles

1. **Schedule your focus.** Reserve blocks of time dedicated solely to your most important work.
2. **Embrace boredom.** Train your mind to resist the pull of easy distraction.
3. **Quit the shallow.** Ruthlessly minimize low-value, reactive tasks.
4. **Rest deliberately.** Recovery is part of the process, not a departure from it.

## A Simple Practice

Begin each day by choosing one task that matters most. Protect a 90-minute window for it, silence your devices, and give it your undivided attention. Over time, these sessions compound into remarkable results.

> "Clarity about what matters provides clarity about what does not."

Deep work is not a productivity hack — it is a way of living intentionally in a distracted world.

content.content:
You are an agent running in the non-interactive mode.
The user is unable to see what you are doing, and cannot answer any questions.
Do not ask questions.
Complete the task before you, and at the end call [completeJob] with the output.
If you are unable to complete the task, call [completeJob] with the failure reason.
If no output is required, call [completeJob] with an empty object: {}
Do not stop until you call [completeJob].
You are running within a test environment.
The prompt is the specification for the test.
Perform the instructions precisely and do not deviate.
Do not fake any work if the provided tools don't work.
The goal is to test the system, so be honest about the results.
If available tools prevented you from completing the task fully, report the failure.
Do not fall back on your own knowledge, only use the tools provided.

## Skills Definitions

<skill>
  You can query available skills and enable them in the current conversation.
  Use [query-skills] to refresh the list of available skills.
  Use [enable-skills] to enable skills by their keys. Always call [query-skills] first.
  Only skills with agentCanEnable=true can be enabled by the agent.
  
  <available_skills>
  -  "Agent skill"
  -  "Agent Wizard" -- Help the user create a new agent (subscriptions, optional cron timer, sync-triggers after edits). [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Alarm" -- Schedule a self-wake and inspect the current time. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Assistant"
  -  "Browser" -- Access to a real isolated browser. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Calendar"
  -  "Connectors" -- Prompt the user to connect external services when a connector is required but unavailable. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Database" -- Query and manipulate objects in the ECHO database. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Delegation" -- Delegates work to sub-agents and tracks it as plan tasks.
  -  "Discord" -- Discord integration.
  -  "Inbox" [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Inbox (Send)"
  -  "Linear" -- Syncs Linear workspaces.
  -  "Markdown" -- Work with markdown documents. Preferred over raw database operations. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Memory" -- Persistent memory storage and retrieval. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Planning" -- Plans and tracks complex tasks using artifacts. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Skill Manager" -- Query and enable skills in the current conversation.
  -  "Triggers" -- Trigger management and automation.
  -  "Web Search" -- Search the web. [agent-can-enable]
  </available_skills>
  
  NOTE: You must enable the skill to use it, only then the tools from that skill will appear.
</skill>

<skill>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</skill>

<skill>
  You can create, read and update markdown documents.
  When asked to edit or update documents return updates as a set of compact diff string pairs.
  For each diff, respond with the smallest possible matching span.
  
  Example:
  ```diff
  - "There is a tyop in this sentence."
  + "There is a typo in this sentence."
  - "This id good."
  + "This sentence is really good."
  ```
</skill>

Draft a new document.
Pick a title and content yourself.

Completion criteria:
- A Markdown document object exists in the database.
- Agent enabled the markdown skill.
- Agent used markdown skill tools to draft a document.

params.name: The Art of Deep Work
params.content:
# The Art of Deep Work

Deep work is the ability to focus without distraction on a cognitively demanding task. In an age of constant notifications and fragmented attention, cultivating this skill has become both increasingly rare and increasingly valuable.

## Why It Matters

- **Quality over quantity**: Sustained focus produces work of higher quality than scattered effort.
- **Skill acquisition**: Mastering hard things requires uninterrupted concentration.
- **Meaning**: Immersion in challenging work is a reliable source of satisfaction.

## Principles

1. **Schedule your focus.** Reserve blocks of time dedicated solely to your most important work.
2. **Embrace boredom.** Train your mind to resist the pull of easy distraction.
3. **Quit the shallow.** Ruthlessly minimize low-value, reactive tasks.
4. **Rest deliberately.** Recovery is part of the process, not a departure from it.

## A Simple Practice

Begin each day by choosing one task that matters most. Protect a 90-minute window for it, silence your devices, and give it your undivided attention. Over time, these sessions compound into remarkable results.

> "Clarity about what matters provides clarity about what does not."

Deep work is not a productivity hack — it is a way of living intentionally in a distracted world.

params.name: The Art of Deep Work
params.content:
# The Art of Deep Work

Deep work is the ability to focus without distraction on a cognitively demanding task. In an age of constant notifications and fragmented attention, cultivating this skill has become both increasingly rare and increasingly valuable.

## Why It Matters

- **Quality over quantity**: Sustained focus produces work of higher quality than scattered effort.
- **Skill acquisition**: Mastering hard things requires uninterrupted concentration.
- **Meaning**: Immersion in challenging work is a reliable source of satisfaction.

## Principles

1. **Schedule your focus.** Reserve blocks of time dedicated solely to your most important work.
2. **Embrace boredom.** Train your mind to resist the pull of easy distraction.
3. **Quit the shallow.** Ruthlessly minimize low-value, reactive tasks.
4. **Rest deliberately.** Recovery is part of the process, not a departure from it.

## A Simple Practice

Begin each day by choosing one task that matters most. Protect a 90-minute window for it, silence your devices, and give it your undivided attention. Over time, these sessions compound into remarkable results.

> "Clarity about what matters provides clarity about what does not."

Deep work is not a productivity hack — it is a way of living intentionally in a distracted world.

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "echo:/YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "echo:/XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

in.description: Scope the query to children of specific objects (transitively). Use this to query items within containers such as feeds or folders. For example, to find emails use `in: [mailbox1.feed, mailbox2.feed]`.

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

content.content:
You are an agent running in the non-interactive mode.
The user is unable to see what you are doing, and cannot answer any questions.
Do not ask questions.
Complete the task before you, and at the end call [completeJob] with the output.
If you are unable to complete the task, call [completeJob] with the failure reason.
If no output is required, call [completeJob] with an empty object: {}
Do not stop until you call [completeJob].
You are running within a test environment.
The prompt is the specification for the test.
Perform the instructions precisely and do not deviate.
Do not fake any work if the provided tools don't work.
The goal is to test the system, so be honest about the results.
If available tools prevented you from completing the task fully, report the failure.
Do not fall back on your own knowledge, only use the tools provided.

## Skills Definitions

<skill>
  You can query available skills and enable them in the current conversation.
  Use [query-skills] to refresh the list of available skills.
  Use [enable-skills] to enable skills by their keys. Always call [query-skills] first.
  Only skills with agentCanEnable=true can be enabled by the agent.
  
  <available_skills>
  -  "Agent skill"
  -  "Agent Wizard" -- Help the user create a new agent (subscriptions, optional cron timer, sync-triggers after edits). [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Alarm" -- Schedule a self-wake and inspect the current time. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Assistant"
  -  "Browser" -- Access to a real isolated browser. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Calendar"
  -  "Connectors" -- Prompt the user to connect external services when a connector is required but unavailable. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Database" -- Query and manipulate objects in the ECHO database. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Delegation" -- Delegates work to sub-agents and tracks it as plan tasks.
  -  "Discord" -- Discord integration.
  -  "Inbox" [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Inbox (Send)"
  -  "Linear" -- Syncs Linear workspaces.
  -  "Markdown" -- Work with markdown documents. Preferred over raw database operations. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Memory" -- Persistent memory storage and retrieval. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Planning" -- Plans and tracks complex tasks using artifacts. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Skill Manager" -- Query and enable skills in the current conversation.
  -  "Triggers" -- Trigger management and automation.
  -  "Web Search" -- Search the web. [agent-can-enable]
  </available_skills>
  
  NOTE: You must enable the skill to use it, only then the tools from that skill will appear.
</skill>

<skill>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</skill>

The database starts empty.

Enable the markdown skill (key: org.dxos.skill.markdown) using the skill manager.

Create a new markdown document named "Empty Notes" with empty content (no body text).

Open the document and confirm its content is empty.

Use the markdown Update operation (org.dxos.function.markdown.update) to append this exact line
without providing oldString (omit oldString entirely — do not pass an empty string):
"Hello from an empty document."

Open the document again and confirm the content is exactly "Hello from an empty document."

Completion criteria:
- Agent enabled the markdown skill successfully.
- A markdown document was created with initially empty content.
- The Update operation completed without error when appending with no oldString.
- Final document content is exactly "Hello from an empty document."
<input>{}</input>

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "echo:/YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "echo:/XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

in.description: Scope the query to children of specific objects (transitively). Use this to query items within containers such as feeds or folders. For example, to find emails use `in: [mailbox1.feed, mailbox2.feed]`.

typename.description: The typename of the objects to list.

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

<skill>
  You can query available skills and enable them in the current conversation.
  Use [query-skills] to refresh the list of available skills.
  Use [enable-skills] to enable skills by their keys. Always call [query-skills] first.
  Only skills with agentCanEnable=true can be enabled by the agent.
  
  <available_skills>
  -  "Agent skill"
  -  "Agent Wizard" -- Help the user create a new agent (subscriptions, optional cron timer, sync-triggers after edits). [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Alarm" -- Schedule a self-wake and inspect the current time. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Assistant"
  -  "Browser" -- Access to a real isolated browser. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Calendar"
  -  "Connectors" -- Prompt the user to connect external services when a connector is required but unavailable. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Database" -- Query and manipulate objects in the ECHO database. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Delegation" -- Delegates work to sub-agents and tracks it as plan tasks.
  -  "Discord" -- Discord integration.
  -  "Inbox" [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Inbox (Send)"
  -  "Linear" -- Syncs Linear workspaces.
  -  "Markdown" -- Work with markdown documents. Preferred over raw database operations. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Memory" -- Persistent memory storage and retrieval. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Planning" -- Plans and tracks complex tasks using artifacts. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Skill Manager" -- Query and enable skills in the current conversation.
  -  "Triggers" -- Trigger management and automation.
  -  "Web Search" -- Search the web. [agent-can-enable]
  </available_skills>
  
  NOTE: You must enable the skill to use it, only then the tools from that skill will appear.
</skill>

<skill>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</skill>

<skill>
  You can create, read and update markdown documents.
  When asked to edit or update documents return updates as a set of compact diff string pairs.
  For each diff, respond with the smallest possible matching span.
  
  Example:
  ```diff
  - "There is a tyop in this sentence."
  + "There is a typo in this sentence."
  - "This id good."
  + "This sentence is really good."
  ```
</skill>
